title: Fill a Form
description: Learn how to interact with form elements including text inputs, checkboxes, and buttons
instructions:
Write a Playwright test that:
1. Navigates to a registration form
2. Fills in name, email, and password fields
3. Checks the terms and conditions checkbox
4. Clicks the submit button
5. Verifies successful submission

starterCode.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_fill_form(page: Page):
    # TODO: Navigate to https://practice.expandtesting.com/form-validation
    
    # TODO: Fill in the contact name field
    
    # TODO: Fill in the contact number field
    
    # TODO: Select pickup date
    
    # TODO: Select payment method
    
    # TODO: Click register button
    pass

solution.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_fill_form(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://practice.expandtesting.com/form-validation')
    page.fill('#validationCustom01', 'John Doe')
    page.fill('#validationCustom05', '1234567890')
    page.fill('#validationCustom04', '2024-12-31')
    page.select_option('#validationCustom03', 'credit-card')
    page.get_by_role('button', name='Register').click()
    expect(page.get_by_text('Form submitted successfully')).to_be_visible()

hints: Use page.fill(selector, value) for text inputs
CSS selectors with # are for IDs
Use page.getByRole('button') to find submit buttons

testCases.name: All fields are filled
testCases.expectedBehavior: All form fields contain the correct values

testCases.name: Success message appears
testCases.expectedBehavior: Form submission success message is visible
